prompt:
Goal: Create a vertical Japanese travel magazine cover for Nagoya, with an airy, nostalgic weekend-trip mood aimed at young women.

Canvas: Portrait magazine cover, 4:5 aspect ratio, full-bleed photographic design. Bright clear blue sky, soft sunlight, clean editorial layout, high-resolution lifestyle travel photography with a slightly dreamy commercial magazine finish.

Main image: Use Nagoya Castle as the large background landmark on the upper-right and center, slightly softened by depth of field but recognizable with white walls and green roofs. In the foreground, place exactly 2 young adult women travelers standing side by side on a bridge or promenade with ornate green railing; both are fashionably dressed in light spring clothing. Woman 1 wears an ivory knit top and long white skirt with a beige shoulder bag, holding a drink or small item. Woman 2 wears a white button-up blouse and pale blue skirt with a beige tote bag. Both faces may be softly obscured or not emphasized. Add lush green trees behind them and a fresh blue-sky atmosphere.

Typography and text content: Make the cover look like a Japanese travel magazine with large elegant white and pink type over the sky. Include exactly 9 main text areas: 1) small top-left line 「週末に行きたい、とっておき名古屋」, 2) huge white title 「名古屋、」, 3) huge pink brush-style title 「ふたり旅。」, 4) English subtitle “NAGOYA TRIP”, 5) top-right magazine label “PHOTO & TRAVEL MAGAZINE” plus “2024-2025” and 「旅の記憶を、一生の宝物に。」, 6) circular stamp near the castle reading “LOVE NAGOYA” with Japanese text 「女子旅で見つけるときめき BEST SPOTS」, 7) left feature block with pink headline 「快晴の城下町さんぽ」 and smaller Japanese copy about Nagoya Castle, Meijo Park, and Osu shopping street, 8) left feature block with pink headline 「映える街角とご当地グルメ」 and smaller Japanese copy about hitsumabushi, miso katsu, and Ogura toast, 9) left feature block with pink headline 「心ときめくフォトジェニック旅」 and smaller Japanese copy about colorful moments and favorite spots. Add a vertical white poetic caption on the right side: 「青空の下で、いちばん素敵な思い出を」. Add a handwritten white script near the lower-right main photo reading “It’s time to discover Nagoya!”

Bottom collage: Along the bottom edge, overlay exactly 4 tilted postcard-style photos with white borders and subtle shadows: 1) Nagoya TV Tower / Oasis 21 style modern city landmark with flowers in foreground, 2) close-up of hitsumabushi eel rice set with side dishes, 3) red Osu shopping arcade entrance with large lanterns reading 「大須」, 4) Ogura toast dessert with red cherry and coffee. Add exactly 4 small tape-label captions around the collage: 「美しい景色と、美味しい時間。」, 「名古屋で見つけた、私たちの特別。」, 「歩いて、食べて、笑って。」, and 「ふたりだから、もっと楽しい。」

Visual style: Japanese women’s travel magazine cover, elegant editorial composition, soft pastel palette of sky blue, white, blush pink, beige, and fresh green. Mix sharp foreground photography with gently blurred background landmark depth. Use refined Mincho-style Japanese typography for the large title, pink brush lettering for the travel phrase, and small neat serif/sans-serif editorial text.

Constraints: Preserve the exact count of 2 women, 4 bottom postcard photos, 9 main text areas, and 4 small collage captions. Keep all Japanese text legible and placed like a real magazine cover. No extra logos, barcodes, QR codes, watermarks, or unrelated text.
